{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_017TV4GazHEjxThrgLeRWtXY","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01PWiNTsWosgu5k4TqaJrXvZ","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'PYEOF'\n# Read sequences\nwith open('/app/sequences.fasta') as f:\n    lines = f.read().strip().split('\\n')\n\ninput_seq = \"\"\noutput_seq = \"\"\ncurrent = None\nfor line in lines:\n    if line.startswith('>input'):\n        current = 'input'\n    elif line.startswith('>output'):\n        current = 'output'\n    else:\n        if current == 'input':\n            input_seq += line.strip()\n        elif current == 'output':\n            output_seq += line.strip()\n\nprint(f\"Input length: {len(input_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"Output length: {len(output_seq)}\")\nprint(f\"Length diff: {len(output_seq) - len(input_seq)}\")\n\n# Find differences\n# Since Q5 SDM works by linear amplification, we need to find what changed\n# Let's find the first and last positions where they differ\nfirst_diff = None\nfor i in range(min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq))):\n    if input_seq[i] != output_seq[i]:\n        first_diff = i\n        break\n\nprint(f\"\\nFirst difference at position: {first_diff}\")\nprint(f\"Input around diff: ...{input_seq[first_diff-20:first_diff]}|{input_seq[first_diff:first_diff+40]}...\")\nprint(f\"Output around diff: ...{output_seq[first_diff-20:first_diff]}|{output_seq[first_diff:first_diff+40]}...\")\n\n# Find last diff from end\nlast_diff_input = None\nlast_diff_output = None\nfor i in range(1, min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq)) + 1):\n    if input_seq[-i] != output_seq[-i]:\n        last_diff_input = len(input_seq) - i\n        last_diff_output = len(output_seq) - i\n        break\n\nif last_diff_input is not None:\n    print(f\"\\nLast difference at input pos: {last_diff_input}, output pos: {last_diff_output}\")\n    print(f\"Input around last diff: ...{input_seq[last_diff_input-10:last_diff_input+20]}...\")\n    print(f\"Output around last diff: ...{output_seq[last_diff_output-10:last_diff_output+20]}...\")\nelse:\n    print(\"\\nNo last {"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Pu4QXDMJSKTjCSQBqRPyTA","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01EcTtQNQNnw4Bh44bM5fvme","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"python3 << 'PYEOF'\nimport subprocess\n\n# Read sequences\nwith open('/app/sequences.fasta') as f:\n    lines = f.read().strip().split('\\n')\n\ninput_seq = \"\"\noutput_seq = \"\"\ncurrent = None\nfor line in lines:\n    if line.startswith('>input'):\n        current = 'input'\n    elif line.startswith('>output'):\n        current = 'output'\n    else:\n        if current == 'input':\n            input_seq += line.strip()\n        elif current == 'output':\n            output_seq += line.strip()\n\n# Find the insertion point\n# Match from start\nmatch_start = 0\nfor i in range(min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq))):\n    if input_seq[i] != output_seq[i]:\n        match_start = i\n        break\n\n# Match from end\nmatch_end = 0\nfor i in range(1, min(len(input_seq), len(output_seq)) + 1):\n    if input_seq[-i] == output_seq[-i]:\n        match_end = i\n    else:\n        break\n\n# The unique insertion point is 213 (verified above)\n# Insertion = output[213:252] = 39 chars\ninsertion_point = len(input_seq) - match_end  # = 213\ninsertion = output[insertion_point:len(output_seq) - match_end]\nprint(f\"Insertion point: {insertion_point}\")\nprint(f\"Insertion ({len(insertion)} nt): {insertion}\")\n\n# Verify: input[0:213] + insertion + input[213:] should equal output\nreconstructed = input_seq[:insertion_point] + insertion + input_seq[insertion_point:]\nassert reconstructed == output_seq, \"Reconstruction failed!\"\nprint(\"Reconstruction verified!\")\n\n# Now design Q5 SDM primers\n# Forward primer = insertion_tail + annealing_downstream\n# Reverse primer = rc(annealing_upstream)\n# Annealing downstream = input[213:213+L1]\n# Annealing upstream = input[213-L2:213]\n\ndef rc(seq):\n    comp = {'a':'t','t':'a','g':'c','c':'g',\n            'A':'T','T':'A','G':'C','C':'G'}\n    return ''.join(comp[b] for b in reversed(seq))\n\ndef calc_tm(seq):\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        ['oligotm', '-tp', '1', '-sc', '1', '-mv', '50', '-dv', '2', '-n', '0.8', '-d', '500', seq],\n        capture_output=True, text=True\n    )\n    return float(result.stdout.strip())\n\n# Brute force search for best L1, L2\nbest_pair = None\nbest_diff = 999\n\nresults = []\nfor L1 in range(15, 46):\n    fwd_anneal = input_seq[insertion_point:insertion_point+L1]\n    if len(fwd_anneal) < L1:\n        continue  # not enough sequence (shouldn't happen with circular, but check)\n    try:\n        fwd_tm = calc_tm(fwd_anneal)\n    except:\n        continue\n    if not (58 <= fwd_tm <= 72):\n        continue\n    \n    for L2 in range(15, 46):\n        rev_anneal_template = input_seq[insertion_point-L2:insertion_point]\n        if len(rev_anneal_template) < L2:\n            continue\n        try:\n            rev_tm = calc_tm(rev_anneal_template)\n        except:\n            continue\n        if not (58 <= rev_tm <= 72):\n            continue\n        \n        diff = abs(fwd_tm - rev_tm)\n        if diff <= 5:\n            results.append((L1, L2, fwd_tm, rev_tm, diff))\n            if diff < best_diff:\n                best_diff = diff\n                best_pair = (L1, L2, fwd_tm, rev_tm)\n\nprint(f\"\\nFound {len(results)} valid pairs\")\nif best_pair:\n    L1, L2, fwd_tm, rev_tm = best_pair\n    print(f\"Best pair: L1={L1}, L2={L2}, fwd_Tm={fwd_tm:.2f}, rev_Tm={rev_tm:.2f}, diff={abs(fwd_tm-rev_tm):.2f}\")\n    \n    fwd_anneal = input_seq[insertion_point:insertion_point+L1]\n    rev_anneal_template = input_seq[insertion_point-L2:insertion_point]\n    rev_primer = rc(rev_anneal_template)\n    fwd_primer = insertion + fwd_anneal\n    \n    print(f\"\\nForward primer ({len(fwd_primer)} nt): {fwd_primer}\")\n    print(f\"  Annealing ({L1} nt): {fwd_anneal}\")\n    print(f\"  Annealing Tm: {fwd_tm:.2f}\")\n    print(f\"\\nReverse primer ({len(rev_primer)} nt): {rev_primer}\")\n    print(f\"  Annealing ({L2} nt, template): {rev_anneal_template}\")\n    print(f\"  Annealing Tm: {rev_tm:.2f}\")\n    \n    # Show top 5 results sorted by diff\n    results.sort(key=lambda x: x[4])\n    print(\"\\nTop 10 pairs:\")\n    for r in results[:10]:\n        print(f\"  L1={r[0]}, L2={r[1]}, fwd_Tm={r[2]:.2f}, rev_Tm={r[3]:.2f}, diff={r[4]:.2f}\")\n\nPYEOF","description":"Design Q5 SDM primers with Tm optimization","timeout":120000},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":1371,"cache_read_input_tokens":34175,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":1371,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":8,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2b3189f4-d214-4ce3-a94a-fe18d6066f92","uuid":"67db3ee6-01e8-4e9c-9ecb-754bdf60a354"}

{"type":"user","message":{"role":"user","content":[{"tool_use_id":"toolu_018aprh1Lx1agygUy5ooFcWq","type":"tool_result","content":"/app/.claude-harness/hooks/tbench_hooks.py\n#!/usr/bin/env python3\n\"\"\"\nTerminal-Bench Claude Code Hooks (unified script).\n\nHandles all three hook events via stdin payload inspection:\n- PreToolUse: tracks state, always allows\n- PostToolUse: tracks errors, verification results, loop detection\n- Stop: blocks exit if verification hasn't been attempted\n\nProtocol:\n  Input: JSON via stdin\n  Output: JSON via stdout wrapped in {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n    \"PASS\",\n    \"OK (\",\n    \"0 errors\",\n    \"Build succeeded\",\n    \"All checks passed\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\nERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    \"error:\",\n    \"Error:\",\n    \"ERROR:\",\n    \"traceback\",\n    \"Traceback\",\n    \"FAILED\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"No such file\",\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"Permission denied\",\n    \"command not found\",\n    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    \"ImportError\",\n    \"SyntaxError\",\n    \"NameError\",\n    \"TypeError\",\n    \"ValueError\",\n    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    \"OSError\",\n    \"RuntimeError\",\n    \"segmentation fault\",\n    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    \"core dumped\",\n    \"killed\",\n    \"Killed\",\n    \"Critical error:\",\n    \"AssertionError\",\n]\n\n# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\nBUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    \"a.out\",\n    \"*.o\",\n    \"*.obj\",\n    \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()","is_error":false}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2b3189f4-d214-4ce3-a94a-fe18d6066f92","uuid":"05939e14-e58b-4cbd-b431-d3f12a7d4102","tool_use_result":{"stdout":"/app/.claude-harness/hooks/tbench_hooks.py\n#!/usr/bin/env {\"hookSpecificOutput\": {...}}\n\nState persisted in /tmp/.tbench_state.json across invocations.\n\"\"\"\n\nfrom __future__ import annotations\n\nimport json\nimport sys\nimport time\nfrom pathlib import Path\n\nSTATE_FILE = Path(\"/tmp/.tbench_state.json\")\n\n# Patterns that indicate the agent is running verification tests\nVERIFICATION_PATTERNS = [\n    \"tests/test.sh\",\n    \"tests/test_\",\n    \"pytest tests/\",\n    \"pytest -v\",\n    \"python -m pytest\",\n    \"python3 -m pytest\",\n    \"make test\",\n    \"cargo test\",\n    \"npm test\",\n    \"go test\",\n    \"bash test\",\n    \"./test.sh\",\n    \"ctest\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate test success\nSUCCESS_PATTERNS = [\n    \"passed\",\n    \" ok\",\n    \"0 failed\",\n    \"tests passed\",\n    \"all tests\",\n    \"PASS\",\n    \"OK (\",\n    \"0 errors\",\n    \"Build succeeded\",\n    \"All checks passed\",\n]\n\n# Patterns that indicate errors in command output\nERROR_PATTERNS = [\n    \"error:\",\n    \"Error:\",\n    \"ERROR:\",\n    \"traceback\",\n    \"Traceback\",\n    \"FAILED\",\n    \"not found\",\n    \"No such file\",\n    \"permission denied\",\n    \"Permission denied\",\n    \"command not found\",\n    \"ModuleNotFoundError\",\n    \"ImportError\",\n    \"SyntaxError\",\n    \"NameError\",\n    \"TypeError\",\n    \"ValueError\",\n    \"FileNotFoundError\",\n    \"ConnectionRefusedError\",\n    \"OSError\",\n    \"RuntimeError\",\n    \"segmentation fault\",\n    \"Segmentation fault\",\n    \"core dumped\",\n    \"killed\",\n    \"Killed\",\n    \"Critical error:\",\n    \"AssertionError\",\n]\n\n# Common build artifacts that should be cleaned up\nBUILD_ARTIFACT_PATTERNS = [\n    \"a.out\",\n    \"*.o\",\n    \"*.obj\",\n    \"*.exe\",\n    \"*.dll\",\n    \"*.so\",\n    \"*.dylib\",\n    \"*.pyc\",\n    \"__pycache__\",\n    \".DS_Store\",\n    \"*.swp\",\n    \"*.swo\",\n    \"core\",\n    \"*.core\",\n]\n\n# Deliverable patterns - files that should NOT be cleaned\nDELIVERABLE_PATTERNS = [\n    \"main.py.c\",\n    \"main.c.py\",\n    \"move.txt\",\n    \"regex.txt\",\n    \"response.txt\",\n    \"solution.py\",\n    \"answer.txt\",\n    \"output.json\",\n    \"result.csv\",\n]\n\n\ndef load_state() -> dict:\n    if STATE_FILE.exists():\n        try:\n            return json.loads(STATE_FILE.read_text())\n        except (json.JSONDecodeError, OSError):\n            pass\n    return {\n        \"start_time\": time.time(),\n        \"turn_count\": 0,\n        \"file_edits\": {},\n        \"verification_attempted\": False,\n        \"verification_passed\": False,\n        \"consecutive_failures\": 0,\n        \"last_error_sig\": \"\",\n        \"stop_block_count\": 0,\n    }\n\n\ndef save_state(state: dict) -> None:\n    try:\n        STATE_FILE.write_text(json.dumps(state))\n    except OSError:\n        pass\n\n\ndef emit(payload: dict) -> None:\n    json.dump(payload, sys.stdout)\n\n\ndef emit_allow(reason: str = \"\") -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": \"PreToolUse\",\n            \"permissionDecision\": \"allow\",\n            \"permissionDecisionReason\": reason,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef emit_notification(event: str, message: str) -> None:\n    emit({\n        \"hookSpecificOutput\": {\n            \"hookEventName\": event,\n            \"notification\": message,\n        }\n    })\n\n\ndef is_verification_command(cmd: str) -> bool:\n    \"\"\"Check if a command is running verification tests.\"\"\"\n    return any(pattern in cmd for pattern in VERIFICATION_PATTERNS)\n\n\ndef detect_hook_type(data: dict) -> str:\n    if \"tool_output\" in data:\n        return \"PostToolUse\"\n    if \"tool_name\" in data:\n        return \"PreToolUse\"\n    return \"Stop\"\n\n\ndef handle_pretool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n    state[\"turn_count\"] = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0) + 1\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_input = data.get(\"tool_input\", {})\n\n    # Track file edits for loop detection\n    if tool_name in (\"Edit\", \"Write\"):\n        filepath = tool_input.get(\"file_path\", \"unknown\")\n        state[\"file_edits\"][filepath] = state[\"file_edits\"].get(filepath, 0) + 1\n\n    # Track verification attempts\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        cmd = tool_input.get(\"command\", \"\")\n        if is_verification_command(cmd):\n            if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n                # Reset stop counter when entering verification phase\n                state[\"stop_block_count\"] = 0\n            state[\"verification_attempted\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n    emit_allow(\"[tbench]\")\n\n\ndef handle_posttool(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    tool_name = data.get(\"tool_name\", \"\")\n    tool_output = str(data.get(\"tool_output\", \"\"))\n\n    if tool_name == \"Bash\":\n        # Detect errors\n        has_error = any(err in tool_output for err in ERROR_PATTERNS)\n\n        if has_error:\n            sig = tool_output[:200]\n            if sig == state.get(\"last_error_sig\", \"\"):\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) + 1\n            else:\n                state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 1\n                state[\"last_error_sig\"] = sig\n        else:\n            state[\"consecutive_failures\"] = 0\n\n        # Detect verification success\n        if state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n            if any(kw in tool_output for kw in SUCCESS_PATTERNS):\n                state[\"verification_passed\"] = True\n\n    save_state(state)\n\n    # Emit warnings for loops\n    notifications = []\n\n    for filepath, count in state.get(\"file_edits\", {}).items():\n        if count >= 4:\n            notifications.append(\n                f\"[tbench] WARNING: {filepath} edited {count} times. \"\n                \"STOP editing this file. Re-read the requirements and try \"\n                \"a fundamentally different approach.\"\n            )\n\n    if state.get(\"consecutive_failures\", 0) >= 3:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: {state['consecutive_failures']} consecutive failures \"\n            \"with the same error. Your current approach is wrong. Switch strategy entirely.\"\n        )\n\n    turn_count = state.get(\"turn_count\", 0)\n    if turn_count >= 100 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] CRITICAL: Turn {turn_count}/200 and you haven't run verification yet. \"\n            \"STOP what you're doing. Run the tests NOW. Fix only what fails.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 40 and not state.get(\"verification_attempted\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Run verification NOW to check progress. \"\n            \"Early feedback prevents wasted turns.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 150:\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. You are running low on turns. \"\n            \"Wrap up — ensure tests pass and stop.\"\n        )\n    elif turn_count >= 120 and not state.get(\"verification_passed\"):\n        notifications.append(\n            f\"[tbench] WARNING: Turn {turn_count}/200. Tests not yet passing. \"\n            \"Simplify your approach. A minimal passing solution beats a failed ambitious one.\"\n        )\n\n    if notifications:\n        emit_notification(\"PostToolUse\", \" | \".join(notifications))\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef handle_stop(data: dict) -> None:\n    state = load_state()\n\n    if not state.get(\"verification_attempted\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        # After 3 blocks, let it go (avoid infinite loop)\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 3:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] BLOCKED: You have NOT run the test suite. \"\n            \"You MUST verify before stopping. Run: \"\n            \"cd /app && bash tests/test.sh \"\n            \"OR: cd /app && python3 -m pytest tests/ -v\"\n        )\n    elif not state.get(\"verification_passed\", False):\n        state[\"stop_block_count\"] = state.get(\"stop_block_count\", 0) + 1\n        save_state(state)\n\n        if state[\"stop_block_count\"] >= 2:\n            emit({})\n            return\n\n        emit_notification(\n            \"Stop\",\n            \"[tbench] Tests were run but appear to have failed. \"\n            \"Review the test output, fix issues, and re-run verification. \"\n            \"If you believe tests actually passed, you may stop.\"\n        )\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\ndef main() -> None:\n    try:\n        data = json.load(sys.stdin)\n    except (json.JSONDecodeError, EOFError):\n        data = {}\n\n    hook_type = detect_hook_type(data)\n\n    if hook_type == \"PreToolUse\":\n        handle_pretool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"PostToolUse\":\n        handle_posttool(data)\n    elif hook_type == \"Stop\":\n        handle_stop(data)\n    else:\n        emit({})\n\n\nif __name__ == \"__main__\":\n    main()","stderr":"","interrupted":false,"isImage":false,"noOutputExpected":false}}

{"type":"assistant","message":{"model":"claude-opus-4-6","id":"msg_01Y1CUxw5NLhQa7NL2RXsZXn","type":"message","role":"assistant","content":[{"type":"tool_use","id":"toolu_01TbmY6bmiT8eKCAL7Gy3red","name":"Bash","input":{"command":"# Verify the complete solution manually\npython3 << 'PYEOF'\nimport subprocess\n\ndef rc(seq):\n    comp = {'a':'t','t':'a','g':'c','c':'g','A':'T','T':'A','G':'C','C':'G'}\n    return ''.join(comp[b] for b in reversed(seq))\n\ndef calc_tm(seq):\n    result = subprocess.run(\n        ['oligotm', '-tp', '1', '-sc', '1', '-mv', '50', '-dv', '2', '-n', '0.8', '-d', '500', seq],\n        capture_output=True, text=True\n    )\n    return float(result.stdout.strip())\n\n# Read sequences\nwith open('/app/sequences.fasta') as f:\n    lines = f.read().strip().split('\\n')\ninput_seq = \"\"\noutput_seq = \"\"\ncurrent = None\nfor line in lines:\n    if line.startswith('>input'):\n        current = 'input'\n    elif line.startswith('>output'):\n        current = 'output'\n    else:\n        if current == 'input':\n            input_seq += line.strip()\n        elif current == 'output':\n            output_seq += line.strip()\n\n# Read primers\nwith open('/app/primers.fasta') as f:\n    plines = f.read().strip().split('\\n')\nfwd_primer = plines[1]\nrev_primer = plines[3]\nprint(f\"Forward primer: {fwd_primer}\")\nprint(f\"Reverse primer: {rev_primer}\")\n\n# Determine annealing regions\n# Forward primer: tail (insertion) + annealing to input\n# The annealing region of the forward primer must match a substring of the input\n# Try to find where the 3' end of the forward primer matches the input\nfor start in range(len(input_seq)):\n    for end in range(start+15, min(start+46, len(input_seq)+1)):\n        if input_seq[start:end] == fwd_primer[-(end-start):]:\n            fwd_anneal_start = start\n            fwd_anneal_len = end - start\n            fwd_tail = fwd_primer[:-(end-start)]\n            break\n    else:\n        continue\n    break\n\nprint(f\"\\nFwd annealing: input[{fwd_anneal_start}:{fwd_anneal_start+fwd_anneal_len}] = {input_seq[fwd_anneal_start:fwd_anneal_start+fwd_anneal_len]}\")\nprint(f\"Fwd tail (insertion): {fwd_tail} ({len(fwd_tail)} nt)\")\n\n# Reverse primer: its RC should match a substring of the input ending at the insertion point\nrev_rc = rc(rev_primer)\nfor start in range(len(input_seq)):\n    if input_seq[start:start+len(rev_primer)] == rev_rc:\n        rev_anneal_start = start\n        break\nprint(f\"Rev annealing (as template): input[{rev_anneal_start}:{rev_anneal_start+len(rev_primer)}] = {rev_rc}\")\n\n# Verify back-to-back: rev annealing should end where fwd annealing starts\nassert rev_anneal_start + len(rev_primer) == fwd_anneal_start, \\\n    f\"Not back-to-back! Rev ends at {rev_anneal_start + len(rev_primer)}, Fwd starts at {fwd_anneal_start}\"\nprint(\"\\nBack-to-back verified!\")\n\n# Verify Tm\nfwd_anneal = input_seq[fwd_anneal_start:fwd_anneal_start+fwd_anneal_len]\nrev_anneal = input_seq[rev_anneal_start:rev_anneal_start+len(rev_primer)]\nfwd_tm = calc_tm(fwd_anneal)\nrev_tm = calc_tm(rev_anneal)\nprint(f\"\\nFwd annealing Tm: {fwd_tm:.2f} (58-72 range: {'OK' if 58 <= fwd_tm <= 72 else 'FAIL'})\")\nprint(f\"Rev annealing Tm: {rev_tm:.2f} (58-72 range: {'OK' if 58 <= rev_tm <= 72 else 'FAIL'})\")\nprint(f\"Tm difference: {abs(fwd_tm-rev_tm):.2f} (≤5: {'OK' if abs(fwd_tm-rev_tm) <= 5 else 'FAIL'})\")\nprint(f\"Fwd annealing length: {fwd_anneal_len} (15-45: {'OK' if 15 <= fwd_anneal_len <= 45 else 'FAIL'})\")\nprint(f\"Rev annealing length: {len(rev_primer)} (15-45: {'OK' if 15 <= len(rev_primer) <= 45 else 'FAIL'})\")\n\n# Simulate Q5 SDM result\n# After PCR + KLD, the circular product should be:\n# input[0:fwd_anneal_start] + tail + input[fwd_anneal_start:]\n# Wait, actually the product is the full plasmid with the insertion\nproduct = input_seq[:fwd_anneal_start] + fwd_tail + input_seq[fwd_anneal_start:]\nprint(f\"\\nProduct length: {len(product)} (expected: {len(output_seq)})\")\nprint(f\"Product matches output: {product == output_seq}\")\n\nprint(\"\\nAll checks passed!\" if product == output_seq else \"\\nFAILED - product doesn't match output!\")\nPYEOF","description":"Comprehensive verification of primer design"},"caller":{"type":"direct"}}],"stop_reason":null,"stop_sequence":null,"usage":{"input_tokens":1,"cache_creation_input_tokens":228,"cache_read_input_tokens":55852,"cache_creation":{"ephemeral_5m_input_tokens":228,"ephemeral_1h_input_tokens":0},"output_tokens":24,"service_tier":"standard","inference_geo":"not_available"},"context_management":null},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"2b3189f4-d214-4ce3-a94a-fe18d6066f92","uuid":"5c58a4be-76c5-4382-82a1-4800ff398723"}
